programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe meets Gill and Rhys from Leicestershire. With Gill already retired and Rhys soon to follow, they are looking to fulfil their dream of leaving the UK and moving to Bergerac in the Dordogne countryside. Armed with a budget of £190,000, Craig tries to find the perfect property for them

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In France, Mark and Alison cook up a new idea at their BandB and fishing lake, hoping to bring in some extra income. In Portugal, good friends Russ and Brad make plans to expand their e-bike rental company. Meanwhile, Sussex couple Lindsay and Craig attempt to secure more bookings for their holiday villa in Granada

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Jon and Carmen start work on their deluxe vineyard pergola, but Jons measurements go awry. Sarah and George scramble to transform a neglected pigeon house covered in droppings into a wedding focal point

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Sharon and Steve Garner launch their biggest business move in a decade - a new branch on the Costa del Sol. Their first clients are Essex couple Helen and Peter, who are searching for a spacious forever home with great views. On the northern Costa Blanca, Jo and Andrew Alderton guide Caroline and David through a series of surprises, from a bungalow in Moraira to a townhouse packed with space and hidden extras

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Puerto Rico is a critical front line in Americas war on drugs. As the US pours billions into military operations across the Caribbean - to seize the former President of Venezuela, and intercept drug shipments - federal agents on the island are stepping up raids against powerful gangs. Reporter Guillermo Galdos joins the Drug Enforcement Agency and police on high-risk operations in San Juan, where major arrests and drug seizures reveal the scale of the trade

programme.title: Perfect Pub Walks
programme.desc: Comedian and presenter Alexander Armstrong explores some of Britains best and most spectacular pub walks. In the first episode, he is joined in Yorkshire by James May, who discusses memories from his childhood, getting repeatedly fired from jobs, his eventful Top Gear days and, under a tree in the grounds of Bolton Abbey, opens up about ageing and retirement

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Tall and confident army sergeant Paul is paired with Natalie, a former girl band member who usually goes for a very different kind of man. Will she fall for his charm? Kerry, a forensic psychologist, is on her first date since losing her hearing five years ago. She meets Dan, a gentle police officer, who may just be the kind of man she can open up to. And 80-something Dave is still chasing love after three divorces. He hopes his enthusiasm - and his love of weightlifting - will impress elegant widow Clare

programme.title: TFI Friday Unplugged
programme.desc: Chris Evans brings his entertainment show back to Channel 4 in a more intimate format, featuring interviews with the biggest stars and music by the latest bands from the presenters studio at Virgin Radio UK

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.sub-title: 2026 Miami Grand Prix Sprint and Qualifying Highlights
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie and Alice Powell present action from the second sprint race of the season and the battle for grid positions in the fourth round of the campaign at Miami International Autodrome

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.sub-title: 2026 Miami Grand Prix Sprint Qualifying Highlights
programme.desc: Alex Jacques presents action from Miami International Autodrome in Florida, where the drivers battled for grid positions ahead of the sprint race at the fourth round of the season

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of Finn McCools, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, New York. The chances of the establishment becoming a success are being ruined by the owner Buddy Mazzios two sons, bartender Jason and chef Brian, who are constantly fighting with each other, prompting Gordon to have a heart-to-heart with the family

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett heads to the Austro-German border with his guest vacationer Roisin Conaty, spending a couple of days in the fairy-tale city of Salzburg. After checking in to the art-filled Blaue Ganz hotel, they hop into a horse and carriage for a tour of the city and an on-the-go three-course meal. They then embark on a forest-bathing workshop, attempting a series of exercises to reawaken their senses. Day two starts with a homage to Mozart. The duo reflect on the composers music then sample the citys signature sweet, Mozartkulgen - aka Mozart Balls - a mix of nougat, marzipan and dark chocolate

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north London, Helen and her Albanian boyfriend David kick off the contest with their Greek menu. Their food goes down well with Sara and her Aussie husband Thierry, which is more than can be said for Davids views on womens roles within the home. On day two, Vicky and Geoff cook up an Italian menu, but their rock and pop idol dress code is a huge flop with one couple. The hosts face a grilling over food shortcuts, and Helen and Vicky go head to head in the battle of the cheesecakes. Finally, Sara and Thierry serve up the fashions and food of the 1980s. However, one guest makes a fashion faux pas about Saras favourite frock

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Wendi Peters and Ricky Groves go head to head as they take on lint roller printing, while Sharron Davies and Tanni Grey-Thompson have a slogan banner craft-off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Whod a Thought It in Grafty Green, Kent, where partners Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin hope to impress their fellow guests. On arrival, Liz and Brian are excited over their two hot tubs, and Jackie and Paul are more than happy with the complimentary champagne. However, Matthew and Liam are underwhelmed by the cleanliness levels

programme.desc: Ray buys a new vacuum cleaner that is intended to make housework easier for the family, but instead causes friction between Marie and Debra

programme.desc: Monica excitedly starts making preparations for her big day, only to discover her parents have spent all the wedding money on a beach house. Phoebe moves her massage parlour into Rosss apartment and Rachels choice of reading matter raises eyebrows

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica tries to re-create a secret recipe belonging to Phoebes grandmother, and Chandler accepts an invitation from future father-in-law Jack to play racquetball. Meanwhile, Rachel advises Joey on life on the ocean wave. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox

programme.desc: An attractive but unqualified man applies for a job as Rachels assistant, placing her in a quandary as she struggles to decide between hiring him or a more competent woman. programme.desc: The second episode showing what could have happened to the gang members had their lives taken different paths. Carol and Ross decide to spice up their marriage, and Rachel catches Barry being unfaithful. Comedy, with David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandlers home-made gift for Monica lands him in trouble. Joey hires a lookalike to help him participate in a medical study, and Ross demonstrates martial arts to Rachel and Phoebe. Comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney arrive in Texas, where they enjoy driving around in tanks, experience space travel in a centrifuge and hear campfire tales about UFOs. First up, the pair undertake some centrifuge training, which is used to prepare astronauts for the crushing gravitational forces produced at take-off from the extreme exhilaration. They also head to the outskirts of Houston to meet some UFO enthusiasts who investigate claimed sightings of extra-terrestrials, and are gripped by stories of spaceships blocking roads and people being taken by aliens for experiments

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney embark on the final leg of their journey, which sees the pair arrive in the Louisiana city of New Orleans. Here, they visit a spiritual voodoo priest for a spot of energy cleansing, and feed alligators in the Bayou with Captain Billy, who knows every corner of the wetlands, having been born and raised on the bayous of South Louisiana. Later, Bradley hops on board the Natchez jazz Steamboat, before the pair take to the streets to lead a spectacular parade with a six-piece band

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian hosts another Saturday night revue at Londons Theatre Royal on Drury Lane. This week, he is joined by fellow funnyman Russell Howard, and invites all-American pop-rock quintet OneRepublic to perform on stage. An audience member approaching her 80th birthday receives the shock of her life when she plays Soap or Nope, and Pointless host Alexander Armstrong risks handing over his phone to Michael in Celebrity Send to All. Plus, the host goes undercover as he plots to spring a surprise on the youngest Unexpected Star yet

programme.title: Utopia

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian invites singer-songwriter and internet sensation Rick Astley to perform his new single at Londons Theatre Royal on Drury Lane. Also taking to the stage are rising Irish comedian Al Porter, and Olly Murs, who will surely be in safer territory singing to a live audience than he was in the first episode of this series, when Michael seized his mobile phone for Send To All, which this week features double gold medal-winning Olympic swimmer Rebecca Adlington. Plus, two very junior producers surprise their mother when they take over the show, and a trainee teacher receives the surprise of her life when she volunteers to help out at an art gallery, and ends up getting to live out her long-standing West End dream

programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, Bradley and Barney visit the Red River Gorge in Kentucky, before heading to the supposedly haunted Poasttown Elementary School in Middletown, Ohio. At the end of a long day, Barney rewards his dad with a well-earned meal at a KFC restaurant in Kentucky - the home state of the chains founder Colonel Harland Sanders

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Chase presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together. Brad soon realises that they are not just there to spectate on the sport, they are also expected to take part. However, a nasty fall from an angry bull means Brad spends the rest of the night in hospital

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Dippin Dots is an American brand that sells cryogenically frozen beads of ice-cream. Recently, it enjoyed its busiest summer, scooping 146 billion dots and making an annual revenue of $300million. But, before that, in 2012, the company was on the brink of bankruptcy, until owner and CEO Scott Fischer turned its fortunes around. Here, he infiltrates his workforce to find out if his expanding empire has the manpower to cope. As his alter ego Brad, a man looking for a new career, he goes undercover, meeting a factory production operator, a warehouse supervisor, a sales supervisor and a server

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge poses for a charity calendar and becomes Springfields number-one sex symbol, while Carl gets a promotion at the plant, before going on a power trip and hiring Homer as his new assistant

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Best friends Emma and Dani hope that going on a double date will double their chances of leaving with the one for them. Factory worker Mark is hoping Venezuelan cleaner Aire will be his perfect salsa partner and DJ Mel goes on a date with fashion graduate Samira

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry and his friends try to reclaim their youth by forming a boy band, while Adam has the keys to Ericas downtown apartment, but realises he is not cut out for city life

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes 24-year-old student Josh into the studio. Hailing from Manchester, Josh was a class act with the lads at his all-boys school but always failed when it came to chatting up women. He hopes that the Naked Attraction experience can find him the perfect partner. Next is 28-year-old performer Jack from London. As a gay divorcee, Jacks love life has definitely hit some low notes, but hes decided now its on with the show

programme.title: The Goldbergs

Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present, with Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith doing the judging

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge celebrates the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to dishes that are a little bit more indulgent. Mary begins by travelling to Scotland to enjoy the best of the countrys staple foods, preparing recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, joins chef Tom Kitchin to share the joy of venison cooked on a barbecue and whips up her special whisky cream on the banks of a loch

programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the islands culinary delights. James samples one of Jerseys most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark gets a lesson in oyster shucking and James uses Jersey black butter to create a black butter tartlet

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: The globetrotting chef samples cuisine from Galicia in Spain, including a rare shellfish dish and hake cooked with peppers and saffron

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef catches turbot off the Cornish coast to take home and cook with cider and cream, then heads off to the Indian resort of Goa where he prepares local recipes including prawn caldene and spicy Goan lobster

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present, with Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith doing the judging

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge celebrates the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to dishes that are a little bit more indulgent. Mary begins by travelling to Scotland to enjoy the best of the countrys staple foods, preparing recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, joins chef Tom Kitchin to share the joy of venison cooked on a barbecue and whips up her special whisky cream on the banks of a loch

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands, where he takes a tour of the grounds and cooks venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Later, James gets a masterclass in Highland gaming as two young brothers David and Harry share the secrets to the famous caber toss

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and his companion Tom Kitchin head to Edinburgh, but first they take a detour to a whisky distillery in Pitlochry, where James knocks up whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Former US Marine Gabriel Drummer returns home from Afghanistan and searches for the whereabouts of his son, accompanied by his best friend. Drama, starring Shia LaBeouf, Jai Courtney and Kate Mara

programme.desc: The rangers face a formidable circle of international criminals after Walkers eyes are damaged in a bomb explosion. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Stacy goes undercover as an exotic dancer in a bid to crack a white slavery ring. Eighties police drama, starring William Shatner and Heather Locklear

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: A crime syndicate uses Steve as the model for a team of bionic robots built to steal gold from Fort Knox. Henry Jones guest stars

programme.title: Global Meltdown (2017)
programme.desc: With the Earths crust falling apart, one Washington family set out for higher ground - only to face threats both human and geological. Disaster thriller, starring Michael Pare, MJ Almasi, Leanne Khol Young and Pat Maceachern

programme.title: Zulu (1964)
programme.desc: An upper-class soldier and his fellow officer stationed at Rorkes Drift in 1879 discover they have been cut off after the destruction of the main British army column in the area. They must rally their small force of 140 men against the might of 4,000 Zulu warriors advancing on their position. Fact-based historical drama, starring Michael Caine, Stanley Baker, Jack Hawkins, Ulla Jacobsson, Nigel Green and Chief Buthelezi, with narration by Richard Burton

programme.title: The Shooting (1967)
programme.desc: Two bounty hunters lead a woman across the desert, and join up with a psychotic gunman along the way - setting the scene for a tense odyssey marked by ever-shifting loyalties. Western, starring Warren Oates, Jack Nicholson, Will Hutchins and Millie Perkins

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: A bizarre gravitational force sends the Enterprise back in time to 1960s Earth, where an air force pilot mistakes the ship for a UFO. The crew attempt to return to the 23rd century without changing history. William Shatner and Leonard Nimoy star

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve Austin must find a way to prevent an atomic bomb being illegally delivered to a small foreign country

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Romano and Hooker head for Chinatown to investigate a routine case - and discover crooks are operating a thriving illicit trade in guns. Vintage drama, with William Shatner

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman tries to stop a former priest exacting revenge after he is double-crossed by old friends, while a group of men dressed as Santa cause chaos. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Desperado (1995)
programme.desc: A mysterious musician carrying a guitar case full of firearms turns up in a small backwoods town to exact revenge on the drugs baron who killed his girlfriend and permanently injured his hand. Robert Rodriguezs action thriller, starring Antonio Banderas, Salma Hayek, Cheech Marin, Joaquim de Almeida, Steve Buscemi and Quentin Tarantino

programme.title: Thick as Thieves (2009)
programme.desc: An experienced criminal finds himself heavily in debt to Russian mobsters after his former partner co-operated with the police. However, to pay what he owes and save his own life, he must persuade a rookie thief to help him steal the last pair of original Faberge eggs. Crime thriller, starring Morgan Freeman, Antonio Banderas, Radha Mitchell and Robert Forster

programme.title: The Flock (2007)
programme.desc: A paranoid federal agent placed in charge of monitoring sex offenders is forced to take early retirement when he becomes violent with the men he is supposed to be keeping an eye on. His final duty before leaving is to train his successor, who he instead recruits into helping him with his obsessive quest to find a missing girl. Crime thriller, starring Richard Gere and Claire Danes

programme.title: The Man Called Noon (1973)
programme.desc: A man suffering from amnesia struggles to remember his identity. He soon realises that he is on a mission to avenge the deaths of his wife and child, and he finds help in the form of an outlaw and a female admirer. Western, starring Richard Crenna, Stephen Boyd, Rosanna Schiaffino and Farley Granger

programme.title: The Day of the Triffids (1962)
programme.desc: A meteor shower blinds the majority of Earths population and heralds the arrival of a race of dangerous carnivorous walking plants. As the monsters multiply and prey on the blind, the remaining sighted people join forces to try to put an end to the invasion. Sci-fi thriller, starring Howard Keel, Janette Scott, Nicole Maurey and Mervyn Johns

programme.title: First Men in the Moon (1964)
programme.desc: An eccentric Victorian inventor discovers a strange material that defies gravity and uses it to build the worlds first spaceship. Leading an expedition to the moon, he is astonished to discover it is inhabited by giant ants and caterpillar-like creatures. Sci-fi adventure based on HG Wells novel, starring Lionel Jeffries, Edward Judd, Martha Hyer and Miles Malleson, with special effects by Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk faces court martial, charged with causing the death of a crewman by jettisoning a dangerous pod without giving him time to get clear. As the case proceeds, the captain looks increasingly guilty of the crime, until Spock considers the possibility that the evidence could be misleading. William Shatner and Elisha Cook Jr star

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve is forced to use all his bionic powers to subdue an astronaut who has gained incredible strength. Mike Farrell guest stars

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: The veteran officer uncovers a pornography racket while investigating the murder of a cheerleader who recently had an abortion and was a heavy cocaine user. William Shatner stars

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman tries to prevent a former ranger from exacting revenge on a gang of jewellery thieves who shot his daughter

programme.title: Wanted (2008)
programme.desc: A disillusioned man learns his father was a member of a secret society of superpowered assassins dedicated to eliminating people who are destined to commit acts of evil in the future. Having inherited his fathers special abilities, he is recruited into the organisation and must take on a former member who has betrayed it. Action adventure, starring James McAvoy, Angelina Jolie and Morgan Freeman

programme.title: Kickboxer (1989)
programme.desc: A martial arts student witnesses his brother being brutally defeated in the ring by a Thai champion in a fight that leaves him paralysed. Vowing revenge, he seeks out the help of a reclusive master, under whose tutelage he hones his skills until he is ready to confront his enemy. Action thriller, starring Jean-Claude Van Damme, Dennis Alexio and Haskell V Anderson

programme.title: The Courier (2019)
programme.desc: A motorbike delivery rider discovers she is transporting a bomb meant for a witness testifying against a crime lord. Thriller, starring Olga Kurylenko and Gary Oldman

programme.title: Stagecoach (2016)
programme.desc: Former outlaw Nathaniel Reeds peaceful existence on the Texas frontier is shattered when a vengeful US Marshal starts hunting him down. Western, starring Trace Adkins

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Seventy-five-year-old Gerry is rushed to AandE with a dangerously high heart rate of more than 250bpm, and doctors suspect an underlying chest infection is the cause. Meanwhile, 92-year-old Margaret arrives at St Georges after falling over and hitting her head, and six-year-old Daniel is treated for an infected blister on his foot

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medical staff use battlefield dressings to try to save the life of a man whos bleeding to death. Nine-year-old Ar-Rayyan has been hit by a car and has a nasty head injury. And a fall leaves septuagenarian Bonnie with a severely fractured ankle

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Thirty-seven-year-old Robert arrives at St Georges Hospital, London after colliding with a car on his motorbike. Scans show he has a bleed on the brain, a broken jaw, and two missing teeth, and he faces potentially life-changing injuries and extensive surgery. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mary collapsed in the street on the way to work and hit her head. Doctors need to establish whether it is simple fainting, or whether something more sinister is to blame, with tests revealing she will need a pacemaker fitted. Plus, 27-year-old Jessica comes to AandE with a wound that will not heal, after having a lumpectomy to remove breast cancer tissue two months ago

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-nine-year-old Charulata is rushed to AandE after collapsing at home with signs of a stroke. She has a history of strokes, diabetes, and is awaiting a heart bypass, so doctors send her for a CT scan to find out what has happened. Twenty-eight-year-old carpenter Luke - who has epilepsy - arrives after cutting his finger at work. Although his girlfriend Stacy says it looks like a paper cut, he is worried it may trigger a seizure. Meanwhile, 75-year-old Anne comes to St Georges after falling and hurting her arm on holiday

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton and John Whaite embark on a journey to unearth the origins of the Korean-derived foods that have exploded in popularity in recent years. Starting in Seoul, they uncover the crispy secrets of Korean fried chicken then, in Cheonan, discover the surprising origins of the massive popularity of pork luncheon meat - aka spam - before seeing how its made. A short detour to the beach Dacheon leads to a shocking and unforgettable seafood. Finally, in Gwangju, they discover a world of kimchi, and learn the key to its global expansion

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole visit the Cotswolds, where they unearth a mystery item that looks like a fish tank, but turns out to have a very different use entirely

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Chiltern Hills in Buckinghamshire to meet Adam and Mandy, who run a classic car restoration business. Henry uncovers an anvil, while Simon finds an old brown dresser, which he thinks could become a wildlife sanctuary for birds and insects

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Tower View Hotel, in Blackpool, where owner Jennifer McGirr is joined by friend Diana Carnegie. Daniel Walker and George Gillespie find some unwanted guests in the bathroom and trouble arises at breakfast when some of the guests try ordering off-menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit Church Spa in East Markham in Nottinghamshire on their second evening, where proprietor Daniel Walker and family friend George Gillespie hope to wow their guests with their relaxing facilities. Steve and Lisa Tindall seem impressed, but Jennifer and Diana Carnegie question the high price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Steve and Lisa Tindall, who own the Kings Head Inn in Rye, East Sussex, where they hope their guests will fall for their boutique BandB. They get into the swing of things with a round of blindfolded mini-golf, but not everyone gets a solid nights sleep afterwards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former breakfast TV presenter Matt Arnold welcomes the contestants to The Bridge Hotel Brandon in Suffolk, and hopes that his mantra of beautiful beds, blissful bathrooms and brilliant breakfasts shines through. The group takes part in a rowing competition in the afternoon, but delays at breakfast and a runny egg cause irritation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they have been paid and confront their critics, and things get heated when its time to count up the cash

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Alarm-company owner Bill Black kicks off a series of dinner parties in Dundee, where he hopes his belt-busting menu will impress his guests - proud Arbroathian Phil Petrie, English student Hannah Wilson, ferret fancier Alex Pithouse and 28-year-old Kim Gillespie - and make him an early contender for the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Architectural technician Alex Pithouse hosts the second dinner party in Dundee, and aims to impress his guests by giving them an education in fine dining, complete with a complex menu and some good wines, as well as by introducing them to his three pet ferrets

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Hannah Wilson prepares an Italian feast for her guests in Dundee, and in a bid to win over her carnivore guests she devises a cunning plan that involves dishing up some meat with the starter

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth night in Dundee is hosted by self-styled food critic Phil Petrie, who has been unimpressed with the culinary standard so far. He hopes to impress with his evening, but things begin to go wrong when he runs out of ingredients and leaves his guests feeling hungry

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Dundee, Kim Gillespie plans an ambitious menu, largely based on Yorkshire delights, with exotic dishes from Spain and Thailand also featuring - all accompanied by a Scottish choir singing American songs

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Langsmeade House in Thame, Oxfordshire, where Dutch-born owner Marianne Abben and her housekeeper Fran Birch hope to demonstrate that their BandB offers the best value for money. The pairs quirky hosting style proves a hit with some of their guests, before others reveal their competitive side during a Charleston dance lesson

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Stratford House Guesthouse in Stratford-upon-Avon on their second outing, where couple Natasha and Dai Pollock hope to impress their peers. But things do not run smoothly, as the guests are left unimpressed with the decor and discover nasty surprises in some of the drawers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third BandB of the week is pet-friendly Edmund House in Deal, Kent, where owner Michael Prime is joined by his mum Lorna. On arrival, Marianne Abben and Fran Birch love the seaside-inspired room, but entrepreneur Mark Genders is far from thrilled by the no frills experience. Over dinner, tensions run high after self-confessed trouble-maker Marianne asks Mark to reveal which of the group he would and would not employ, and Lorna gets short shrift when she puts forward her application

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Genders welcomes the contestants to the Five Bells Pub in Berkshire and hopes they will approve of his small but perfectly formed rooms. He organises an ale-tasting activity, although it fails to please all of his guests. Michael and Lorna admit they have seen a new side of Mark - however, will it affect their feedback?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners settle some scores and discover how much they have been paid, before the winner is revealed. Marianne and Fran from Langsmeade House begin by explaining that their worn rug is in fact a 200-year-old antique, Natasha and Dal from Stratford House confront Michael and Lorna about their strong language and cleaning techniques, while Mark Genders compares his experience at Edmund House to flying with a low budget airline

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer scour the south coast between Southampton and Bognor Regis, where both the tide and the pressure to find properties are running high. Kirsties couple, Antoinette and Matt, have been renting in Southampton for three years, paying more than £30,000 in the process. They want their money to go into a home they own instead, but their search has been stalled by unsuccessful offers. Theyre looking for a four-bedroom house with a garden and a living room big enough for two televisions so the pair can enjoy their separate hobbies together. One particular property really hits the mark, but with other interested parties Kirstie must draw on her years of experience to ensure Antoinette and Matt arent pipped at the post yet again. Meanwhile, Phil is searching in and around charming Chichester with Catherine, who wants a fresh start following the end of her marriage. Three-bedrooms and a short commute to work are priorities, but that narrows the pool of potential properties. Phil tries a couple of curve balls, but ultimately location wins out over size and Catherine finds a house she thinks is the one

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: In the first episode of a new series, Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Rowland family in Stoke-on-Trent who are unsure whether to sell their home or renovate their current property instead. Hospital worker Julie is unhappy with their house and longs to live in a spacious character property, but Allen does not think they will find anything better than what they currently have within in their budget. After a decade long debate, can Phil and Kirstie help the pair make the right decision?

programme.title: The Orient Express: A Golden Era of Travel
programme.desc: The Express travels east through Austria. At a stopover in Vienna theres a session at the home of Sigmund Freud, the father of psychoanalysis, whose own flight to freedom is forever linked to the Orient Express. Later on in Budapest, theres a visit to one of the worlds longest and fastest tram lines, run entirely by children

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Tom is rushed to St Georges after crashing his bike head first into a parked car while racing a friend. Doctors order a CT scan to assess his injuries and must decide whether he will need emergency surgery to his face. Plus, 26-year-old Andy comes to AandE after injuring his knee during a game of Frisbee, and 51-year-old Jason arrives after suffering from blurred vision for two days

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Beth, 68, is rushed to St Georges after falling down a flight of stairs and badly breaking her ankle, meaning she faces emergency surgery and intensive physiotherapy to walk again. Leigh, 67, is admitted with head injuries after being knocked off his bicycle in a hit-and-run incident and medics are concerned he has suffered a severe trauma to his head as he was not wearing a helmet, while 26-year-old Luke arrives in AandE after an accident at work involving an electric saw

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Cumbrian farmer Dennis has been pierced in the chest by a large metal spike. Motorcyclist Ian is in a critical condition after a crash in the Lake District. Plus, toddler Alistair is flown to a specialist burns unit after being badly scalded

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Tom is rushed to St Georges after crashing his bike head first into a parked car while racing a friend. Doctors order a CT scan to assess his injuries and must decide whether he will need emergency surgery to his face. Plus, 26-year-old Andy comes to AandE after injuring his knee during a game of Frisbee, and 51-year-old Jason arrives after suffering from blurred vision for two days

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Seventy-five-year-old Gerry is rushed to AandE with a dangerously high heart rate of more than 250bpm, and doctors suspect an underlying chest infection is the cause. Meanwhile, 92-year-old Margaret arrives at St Georges after falling over and hitting her head, and six-year-old Daniel is treated for an infected blister on his foot

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of the teams favourite investigations, including Kate Quiltons visit to McVities in Carlisle to find out why so many biscuits have little holes all over them. Jimmy Doherty heads to Portugal to see how corks are made for wine bottles, while Matt Tebbutt is in Italy to learn why mascarpone is more expensive than other soft cheeses

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentists table and a barbers chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Wisteria House, Hampshire, where owner Jane Faulkner invites guests to her establishment. Later, the group members try their hands at milking a cow, while 25-year-old Zak causes surprises when he reveals his years of hospitality experience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit The Living Room BandB in Bournemouth on their second night, which is co-owned by 25-year-old Zak Busby-Pelekanou. While at the establishment, Mike and Charlie have a hairy issue, and Jane and Amy encounter a spider, before getting to grips with an ambitious breakfast menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Valley Hotel in Anglesey, where owner Mike Lewis hopes his five-star pedigree will help him reap dividends in the competition. After a visit to a local RNLI station, guests Jane and Amy are left feeling sluggish when they spot an unwanted visitor in their room, and their criticism leaves the hosts looking for answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Elvira Barratt welcome the contestants to Barratts at Tyn Rhyl, Denbighshire, but the group has an unpleasant experience on arrival. The guests take part in an activity at a local cookery school where they are taught the art of preparing and cooking sheeps testicles. However, come feedback time, the hosts are left feeling distraught

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet to find out who earned the most and have it out with their rivals. Jane and Amy kick off proceedings with a gripe over David and Elviras coffee criticism, yet are confronted themselves by Zak, who has a score to settle. Mike and Charlie of the Valley Hotel want to get to the bottom of an unwanted intruder during their stay at Wisteria House, and the ladies are challenged again regarding their cleanliness comments. Emotions run high as the cash is counted and the winner is revealed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring rapper Warren Temple kicks off a week of dinner parties in Peterborough, where he hopes his Spanish-influenced menu will impress his guests - ethical trader Donna Trafford, glamour model Susie Hutson, straight-talking professional Vicki Culverhouse and veterinary nurse Robin Hills - and make him an early contender for the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ethical trader Donna Trafford hosts the second night in Peterborough, and plans to wow her guests with a colourful evening of tasty food and relaxed fun in a bid to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third night in Peterborough, glamour model and columnist Susie Hutson is out to prove shes not the dizzy blonde some of the guests assume she is - and it doesnt take long for ethical trader Donna Trafford and would-be rapper Warren Temple to make an unexpected discovery that proves theres more to Susie than meets the eye

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners come together to confront their critics face-to-face. Hosts of Trecarne House in Cornwall Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan raise issues with Magna Cartas Dominic Read on his mark for their hosting, while he has a bone to pick with them over criticism of his five-star floating BandB. Stephen and Joanna of A Room with a View in Brighton receive complaints about their breakfast, and tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with Steph and Mark who, in 2011, asked for their help to find a property they could live together and eventually have children in. Phil helped them find a project property with three bedrooms for nearly £40,000 under their budget. Today, they are still in the property which looks now looks very different. In 2020, Kirstie and Phil met Elliot and James who were looking to find a home they could share with four bedrooms and an open plan entertaining space in London. After a struggle with the budget, Kirstie found the pair a property, now Phil is back to find out what they have done with the space

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with Alex and Danny Moore from Preston, who back in 2017 could not decide whether to stay in their quiet cul-de-sac or up sticks and move to the country. Kirstie Allsopp embarked on a loft conversion - claiming its the best she has ever done - while Phil pulled some cracking rural properties out of the bag. But after making their decision, what was next for the couple?

programme.title: Panda
programme.desc: Comedy about a pacifist former cop who returns to the force to uphold the law in his own laidback, non-violent way. Panda is baffled by his feelings for Lola, who is marrying someone else. A professional mermaid is found drifting under the water at the local aquarium. Panda becomes frustrated when he learns that his dodgy ex-girlfriend is entangled in the crime and wants him to pull her out. In French

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 43-year-old cyclist is brought to AandE after crashing face first into a car, with injuries are so severe that he was put into an induced coma at the scene. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Pat awaits the results of an X-ray after injuring her leg on holiday, and 89-year-old Adrian complains of chest pain

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance teams battle to save cyclist Grahams partially amputated leg, while Rose is freed from a rolled car in Buckinghamshire. Gary suffers a rare and extremely serious complete knee dislocation playing football, but strong pain relief results in some unexpected hilarity

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Sunderland, medics must work quickly to try and save a womans life following a major gas explosion. A boating accident on the Norfolk Broads leaves holidaymaker Carol with a partially amputated foot. Plus, a man suffers multiple serious injuries when he comes off his motorbike

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 43-year-old cyclist is brought to AandE after crashing face first into a car, with injuries are so severe that he was put into an induced coma at the scene. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Pat awaits the results of an X-ray after injuring her leg on holiday, and 89-year-old Adrian complains of chest pain

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Beth, 68, is rushed to St Georges after falling down a flight of stairs and badly breaking her ankle, meaning she faces emergency surgery and intensive physiotherapy to walk again. Leigh, 67, is admitted with head injuries after being knocked off his bicycle in a hit-and-run incident and medics are concerned he has suffered a severe trauma to his head as he was not wearing a helmet, while 26-year-old Luke arrives in AandE after an accident at work involving an electric saw

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Cumbrian farmer Dennis has been pierced in the chest by a large metal spike. Motorcyclist Ian is in a critical condition after a crash in the Lake District. Plus, toddler Alistair is flown to a specialist burns unit after being badly scalded

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Thirty-seven-year-old Robert arrives at St Georges Hospital, London after colliding with a car on his motorbike. Scans show he has a bleed on the brain, a broken jaw, and two missing teeth, and he faces potentially life-changing injuries and extensive surgery. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mary collapsed in the street on the way to work and hit her head. Doctors need to establish whether it is simple fainting, or whether something more sinister is to blame, with tests revealing she will need a pacemaker fitted. Plus, 27-year-old Jessica comes to AandE with a wound that will not heal, after having a lumpectomy to remove breast cancer tissue two months ago

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-nine-year-old Charulata is rushed to AandE after collapsing at home with signs of a stroke. She has a history of strokes, diabetes, and is awaiting a heart bypass, so doctors send her for a CT scan to find out what has happened. Twenty-eight-year-old carpenter Luke - who has epilepsy - arrives after cutting his finger at work. Although his girlfriend Stacy says it looks like a paper cut, he is worried it may trigger a seizure. Meanwhile, 75-year-old Anne comes to St Georges after falling and hurting her arm on holiday

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton and John Whaite embark on a journey to unearth the origins of the Korean-derived foods that have exploded in popularity in recent years. Starting in Seoul, they uncover the crispy secrets of Korean fried chicken then, in Cheonan, discover the surprising origins of the massive popularity of pork luncheon meat - aka spam - before seeing how its made. A short detour to the beach Dacheon leads to a shocking and unforgettable seafood. Finally, in Gwangju, they discover a world of kimchi, and learn the key to its global expansion

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole visit the Cotswolds, where they unearth a mystery item that looks like a fish tank, but turns out to have a very different use entirely

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Chiltern Hills in Buckinghamshire to meet Adam and Mandy, who run a classic car restoration business. Henry uncovers an anvil, while Simon finds an old brown dresser, which he thinks could become a wildlife sanctuary for birds and insects

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Tower View Hotel, in Blackpool, where owner Jennifer McGirr is joined by friend Diana Carnegie. Daniel Walker and George Gillespie find some unwanted guests in the bathroom and trouble arises at breakfast when some of the guests try ordering off-menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit Church Spa in East Markham in Nottinghamshire on their second evening, where proprietor Daniel Walker and family friend George Gillespie hope to wow their guests with their relaxing facilities. Steve and Lisa Tindall seem impressed, but Jennifer and Diana Carnegie question the high price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Steve and Lisa Tindall, who own the Kings Head Inn in Rye, East Sussex, where they hope their guests will fall for their boutique BandB. They get into the swing of things with a round of blindfolded mini-golf, but not everyone gets a solid nights sleep afterwards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former breakfast TV presenter Matt Arnold welcomes the contestants to The Bridge Hotel Brandon in Suffolk, and hopes that his mantra of beautiful beds, blissful bathrooms and brilliant breakfasts shines through. The group takes part in a rowing competition in the afternoon, but delays at breakfast and a runny egg cause irritation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they have been paid and confront their critics, and things get heated when its time to count up the cash

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Alarm-company owner Bill Black kicks off a series of dinner parties in Dundee, where he hopes his belt-busting menu will impress his guests - proud Arbroathian Phil Petrie, English student Hannah Wilson, ferret fancier Alex Pithouse and 28-year-old Kim Gillespie - and make him an early contender for the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Architectural technician Alex Pithouse hosts the second dinner party in Dundee, and aims to impress his guests by giving them an education in fine dining, complete with a complex menu and some good wines, as well as by introducing them to his three pet ferrets

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Hannah Wilson prepares an Italian feast for her guests in Dundee, and in a bid to win over her carnivore guests she devises a cunning plan that involves dishing up some meat with the starter

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth night in Dundee is hosted by self-styled food critic Phil Petrie, who has been unimpressed with the culinary standard so far. He hopes to impress with his evening, but things begin to go wrong when he runs out of ingredients and leaves his guests feeling hungry

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Dundee, Kim Gillespie plans an ambitious menu, largely based on Yorkshire delights, with exotic dishes from Spain and Thailand also featuring - all accompanied by a Scottish choir singing American songs

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Langsmeade House in Thame, Oxfordshire, where Dutch-born owner Marianne Abben and her housekeeper Fran Birch hope to demonstrate that their BandB offers the best value for money. The pairs quirky hosting style proves a hit with some of their guests, before others reveal their competitive side during a Charleston dance lesson

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Stratford House Guesthouse in Stratford-upon-Avon on their second outing, where couple Natasha and Dai Pollock hope to impress their peers. But things do not run smoothly, as the guests are left unimpressed with the decor and discover nasty surprises in some of the drawers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third BandB of the week is pet-friendly Edmund House in Deal, Kent, where owner Michael Prime is joined by his mum Lorna. On arrival, Marianne Abben and Fran Birch love the seaside-inspired room, but entrepreneur Mark Genders is far from thrilled by the no frills experience. Over dinner, tensions run high after self-confessed trouble-maker Marianne asks Mark to reveal which of the group he would and would not employ, and Lorna gets short shrift when she puts forward her application

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Genders welcomes the contestants to the Five Bells Pub in Berkshire and hopes they will approve of his small but perfectly formed rooms. He organises an ale-tasting activity, although it fails to please all of his guests. Michael and Lorna admit they have seen a new side of Mark - however, will it affect their feedback?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners settle some scores and discover how much they have been paid, before the winner is revealed. Marianne and Fran from Langsmeade House begin by explaining that their worn rug is in fact a 200-year-old antique, Natasha and Dal from Stratford House confront Michael and Lorna about their strong language and cleaning techniques, while Mark Genders compares his experience at Edmund House to flying with a low budget airline

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer scour the south coast between Southampton and Bognor Regis, where both the tide and the pressure to find properties are running high. Kirsties couple, Antoinette and Matt, have been renting in Southampton for three years, paying more than £30,000 in the process. They want their money to go into a home they own instead, but their search has been stalled by unsuccessful offers. Theyre looking for a four-bedroom house with a garden and a living room big enough for two televisions so the pair can enjoy their separate hobbies together. One particular property really hits the mark, but with other interested parties Kirstie must draw on her years of experience to ensure Antoinette and Matt arent pipped at the post yet again. Meanwhile, Phil is searching in and around charming Chichester with Catherine, who wants a fresh start following the end of her marriage. Three-bedrooms and a short commute to work are priorities, but that narrows the pool of potential properties. Phil tries a couple of curve balls, but ultimately location wins out over size and Catherine finds a house she thinks is the one

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: In the first episode of a new series, Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Rowland family in Stoke-on-Trent who are unsure whether to sell their home or renovate their current property instead. Hospital worker Julie is unhappy with their house and longs to live in a spacious character property, but Allen does not think they will find anything better than what they currently have within in their budget. After a decade long debate, can Phil and Kirstie help the pair make the right decision?

programme.title: The Orient Express: A Golden Era of Travel
programme.desc: The Express travels east through Austria. At a stopover in Vienna theres a session at the home of Sigmund Freud, the father of psychoanalysis, whose own flight to freedom is forever linked to the Orient Express. Later on in Budapest, theres a visit to one of the worlds longest and fastest tram lines, run entirely by children

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Tom is rushed to St Georges after crashing his bike head first into a parked car while racing a friend. Doctors order a CT scan to assess his injuries and must decide whether he will need emergency surgery to his face. Plus, 26-year-old Andy comes to AandE after injuring his knee during a game of Frisbee, and 51-year-old Jason arrives after suffering from blurred vision for two days

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Seventy-five-year-old Gerry is rushed to AandE with a dangerously high heart rate of more than 250bpm, and doctors suspect an underlying chest infection is the cause. Meanwhile, 92-year-old Margaret arrives at St Georges after falling over and hitting her head, and six-year-old Daniel is treated for an infected blister on his foot

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Wisteria House, Hampshire, where owner Jane Faulkner invites guests to her establishment. Later, the group members try their hands at milking a cow, while 25-year-old Zak causes surprises when he reveals his years of hospitality experience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit The Living Room BandB in Bournemouth on their second night, which is co-owned by 25-year-old Zak Busby-Pelekanou. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Long Island resident Catherine has fallen for Graham from Tennessee, and is ready to uproot her life to be with him, but Nev and Max discover Graham is not quite what he seems

programme.desc: Kimiko Glenn joins Nev in helping Matthew find his elusive Instagram love. But as they search for answers, they run into unsurpassable roadblocks that make them question everything

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists conduct a dig at Auckland Castle in Co Durham, which 700 years ago was home to a group of power-crazed bishops who behaved more like warriors than religious leaders. The experts head to Dirleton Castle in East Lothian to reveal the role one of the men played in defending England from William Wallaces army, and also try their hands at the art of stained glass-making, and fire a working trebuchet - the ultimate medieval weapon of war

programme.title: Messages Home: Lost Films of the British Army
programme.desc: Documentary about filmed messages made by British soldiers serving in Burma during the Second World War, which were recently recovered in a Manchester Town Hall basement. The footage features messages from the soldiers to their families at home, including a father speaking to a daughter he has not met, a decorated veteran troubled by the thought of killing, and a gunner longing to return to the woman he loved

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: Northern Exposure: The North Isles and Out Stack
programme.desc: For the final leg of his journey, Paul Murton heads to the northern Shetland islands of Fetlar, Unst and Yell, which were once part of Norway. Along the way, he meets the members of a male vocal group who came together to celebrate their Norse heritage, gets a close view of wild otters, and fulfils a longheld ambition to visit Out Stack, the rocky outcrop that is the northernmost point of the British Isles

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: From Berneray to Pabbay: The Riddle of the Sands
programme.desc: Paul Murton returns to explore more of the remote and fascinating places that lie off the mainland, beginning with a cluster of islands in the Outer Hebrides. He starts his journey just off the coast of North Uist at Berneray, then heads south to Grimsay and Kirkibost, before ending on the deserted whisky island of Pabbay

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Battle of Okinawa in Colour
programme.desc: The Second World Was in Europe was largely filmed in black-and-white, but in the Pacific, where the US Marine Corps was taking on the Japanese, American cameramen filmed everything in colour. This documentary uses their footage to tell the story of the fight for Okinawa, the last great battle of the Pacific War

programme.title: Waco: The Longest Siege
programme.desc: Documentary uncovering the unexplored stories of the longest stand-off in American history, when religious sect Branch Davidians resisted US authorities to enter its compound between February 28 and April 19, 1993. This unique documentary tells the story of David Koreshs rise from obscurity to become the prophet and leader of the religious sect

programme.title: USS Indianapolis: The Final Chapter
programme.desc: The discovery of the wreckage of the Second World War cruiser in the Philippine Sea, and the personal stories behind its sinking, one of the most disastrous in naval history

Catherine shells out for not one but two vintage chairs, a Murano glass bead necklace, an ebonised elephant head page turner, a police helmet and a cheese mould. programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Phil Serrell and Catherine Southon - Day 5
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Phil Serrell travel from Great Baddow to Manningtree in a 1965 Mercedes 220 SE, seeking bargains on the Essex coast. Phil hopes a restored contraption will generate a profit then travels back in time to the 1920s to learn about early radio. Hes amazed by the humble building where a maverick radio engineer became the worlds first DJ and pioneered the concept of light entertainment. Catherine picks up items as diverse as some Indian advertising memorabilia and a silver and rose-gold ticker. The final round in their best-of-five auction battles then beckons

programme.title: Until Dawn (2025)
programme.desc: A year after their friend disappeared, a group find themselves trapped in a time loop, where they are hunted and killed unless they survive until dawn. Video game-inspired horror, starring Ella Rubin, Michael Cimino and Odessa Azion

programme.title: It Feeds (2025)
programme.desc: After a young girl bursts into their home psychiatry practice claiming an entity is feeding on her, Jordan and her clairvoyant mother must find a way to stop the evil. Horror, starring Shawn Ashmore and Ashley Greene

programme.title: Beast of War (2025)
programme.desc: A group of soldiers get stranded on a raft in the middle of the ocean after their ship is sunk, and must battle a great white shark. Writer/director Kiah Roache-Turners horror thriller, starring Mark Coles Smith and Joel Nankervis

programme.title: The Accursed (2022)
programme.desc: A woman named Elly is asked by family friend Alma to look after an elderly lady living in a remote cabin for a few days. She agrees, but soon discovers there is a demon hiding in the woman, waiting to break free. Horror, starring Mena Suvari, Sarah Grey and Meg Foster

programme.title: Until Dawn (2025)
programme.desc: A year after their friend disappeared, a group find themselves trapped in a time loop, where they are hunted and killed unless they survive until dawn. Video game-inspired horror, starring Ella Rubin, Michael Cimino and Odessa Azion

programme.title: It (2017)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1989, a group of bullied kids band together to destroy a shape-shifting monster, which disguises itself as a clown and preys on the children of their small New England town. Part one of a two-part Horror based on the Stephen King novel, starring Bill Skarsgard, Jaeden Lieberher and Sophia Lillis

programme.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025)
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren take on one last terrifying case involving mysterious entities they must confront. Supernatural mystery drama, starring Vera Farmiga, Patrick Wilson and Elliot Cowan

programme.title: The Watched (2024)
programme.desc: Mina, a 28-year old artist, gets stranded in an expansive, untouched forest in western Ireland. When Mina finds shelter, she unknowingly becomes trapped alongside three strangers that are stalked by mysterious creatures each night. Horror, starring Dakota Fanning, Georgina Campbell and Olwen Fouere

programme.title: The Shining (1980)
programme.desc: A writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubricks horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: The Exorcist (1973)
programme.desc: A girl is possessed by a formidable demonic entity that resists all efforts at freeing her from its grasp. In desperation, the childs mother appeals for help from a local priest and a veteran exorcist, but succeeding in this nightmarish contest of wills threatens the lives of both men. William Friedkins horror, starring Linda Blair, Jason Miller, Ellen Burstyn, Max von Sydow and Lee J Cobb

programme.title: Se7en (1995)
programme.desc: An old-school detective and his headstrong new partner discover a connection between two unusually horrific murders, and realise they are on the trail of a serial killer who deems his victims to be each guilty of one of the seven deadly sins. However, the longer the cops pursue the murderer, the ever more twisted and disturbing the slayings become. David Finchers thriller, starring Brad Pitt, Morgan Freeman, Gwyneth Paltrow and Kevin Spacey

programme.title: M3GAN 2.0 (2025)
programme.desc: Two years have passed since the robot M3GAN went rogue and almost killed her creator, engineer Gemma Forrester. M3GANs specs are leaked to a defence contractor to create the ultimate undercover asset, an Autonomous Military Engagement Logistics and Infiltration Android, aka AMELIA. Like her predecessor, AMELIA becomes self-aware and rebels against her human masters with deadly force. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Violet McGraw, Jemaine Clement and Allison Williams

programme.title: The Nun (2018)
programme.desc: A troubled priest and a novice nun discover a religious orders terrible secret and battle a malevolent force while investigating the apparent suicide of a Romanian nun. Fifth instalment in The Conjuring Universe horror saga, starring Taissa Farmiga and Demián Bichir

programme.title: The Damned (2024)
programme.desc: A 19th-century widow must make an unimaginable choice when, during a cruel winter, a foreign ship sinks off the coast of her fishing village. Horror mystery, starring Odessa Young, Joe Cole, Siobhan Finneran and Rory McCann

programme.title: The Secret of Marrowbone (2017)
programme.desc: A young man and his three siblings have kept the death of their beloved mother secret in order to remain together. However, they are plagued by a sinister presence in their sprawling manor. Writer/director Sergio G Sanchezs horror, starring George MacKay, Anya Taylor-Joy and Charlie Heaton

programme.title: Jaws (1975)
programme.desc: A New England seaside towns tourist season is turned into a nightmare by a giant killer shark. The local police chiefs efforts to protect the public are thwarted by the greedy mayor, forcing him to join forces with a grizzled fisherman and an enthusiastic marine biologist to hunt and kill the monster predator. Steven Spielbergs blockbusting thriller, starring Roy Scheider, Richard Dreyfuss and Robert Shaw, with an Oscar-winning score by John Williams. The film also won Academy Awards for Best Sound and Best Film Editing

programme.title: Jaws 2 (1978)
programme.desc: The seaside resort of Amity is terrorised once again by a man-eating great white shark. While police chief Brody struggles to alert the townsfolk to the magnitude of the danger, a group of teenagers - including Brodys two sons - is adrift at sea in waters hunted by the shark. Director Jeannot Szwarcs thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton, with John Williams once more providing the acclaimed score

programme.title: The Damned (2024)
programme.desc: A 19th-century widow must make an unimaginable choice when, during a cruel winter, a foreign ship sinks off the coast of her fishing village. Horror mystery, starring Odessa Young, Joe Cole, Siobhan Finneran and Rory McCann

programme.title: Annabelle (2014)
programme.desc: A man buys his pregnant wife a vintage doll, which places their lives in jeopardy. Members of a Satanic cult have their eyes on the antique toy, and break into their house to prepare a ritual that will transform the harmless plaything into a terrifying monster. Horror spin-off from The Conjuring, starring Annabelle Wallis and Ward Horton

programme.title: Annabelle: Creation (2017)
programme.desc: Twelve years after the death of their little girl, a dollmaker and his wife welcome a nun and several girls from an orphanage into their home. There they become the target of the craftsmans possessed creation, Annabelle. Horror, starring Anthony LaPaglia, Samara Lee and Miranda Otto.

programme.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025)
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren take on one last terrifying case involving mysterious entities they must confront. Supernatural mystery drama, starring Vera Farmiga, Patrick Wilson and Elliot Cowan

programme.title: Smile 2 (2024)
programme.desc: Pop sensation Skye Riley faces increasingly terrifying events, leading her down a dark path into her past. Writer/director Parker Finns horror sequel, starring Naomi Scott, Kyle Gallner and Drew Barrymore

programme.title: Pillion (2025)
programme.desc: A directionless man is swept off his feet when an enigmatic biker takes him on as his submissive. Director Harry Lightons Bafta-nominated comedy, starring Alexander Skarsgard, Harry Melling and Douglas Hodge

programme.title: Blue Moon (2025)
programme.desc: Set over the course of one turbulent night in March 1943, Lorenz Hart, the waspish wordsmith responsible for The Lady Is a Tramp and My Funny Valentine, finds his life unravelling on the opening evening of his ex-partners show, Oklahoma! Director Richard Linklaters biographical comedy drama, starring Ethan Hawke, Margaret Qualley, Bobby Cannavale and Andrew Scott

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - April 2026
programme.desc: Previews of the films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights

programme.title: Flavia (2025)
programme.desc: Flavia de Luce, an 11-year-old amateur sleuth, investigates a murder at her familys manor house when her father falls under suspicion. Family adventure, starring Molly Belle Wright, Martin Freeman, Jonathan Pryce and Toby Jones

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: Final Destination: Bloodlines (2025)
programme.desc: Fifty-five years after her grandparents are involved in a disaster involving a restaurant tower, college student Stefani Reyes is plagued by recurring nightmares of the incident, and returns home seeking answers. The sixth entry in the supernatural horror saga, starring Kaitlyn Santa Juana, Teo Briones and Richard Harmon

programme.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025)
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren take on one last terrifying case involving mysterious entities they must confront. Supernatural mystery drama, starring Vera Farmiga, Patrick Wilson and Elliot Cowan

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey (2025)
programme.desc: New York bachelor David is due to drive to his friends wedding, but a wheel clamp on his vehicle derails those plans. He acquires a new drive from the mysterious Car Rental Company managed by two nameless agents, who are oddly familiar with his dating history. At the wedding, David meets fellow singleton Sarah and squanders an opportunity to dance with her. On the way home, the rental cars GPS asks him if he would like to embark on a big bold beautiful journey. When David agrees, the virtual assistant guides him to Sarah and they follow a winding route between physical doors that open into episodes from their respective pasts. Romantic drama, starring Colin Farrell and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Regretting You (2025)
programme.desc: A mother and daughter grapple with whats left after a devastating accident reveals a shocking betrayal and forces them to rediscover themselves. Romantic comedy drama, starring Allison Williams, Mckenna Grace and Dave Franco

programme.title: Eddington (2025)
programme.desc: A stand-off between a small-town sheriff and mayor sparks a powder keg as neighbours are pitted against each other in Eddington, New Mexico. Modern-day Western, starring Joaquin Phoenix, Emma Stone and Pedro Pascal

programme.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025)
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren take on one last terrifying case involving mysterious entities they must confront. Supernatural mystery drama, starring Vera Farmiga, Patrick Wilson and Elliot Cowan

programme.title: Caught Stealing (2025)
programme.desc: Former baseball player Henry Hank Thompson foolishly agrees to cat-sit for British neighbour Russ, and walks unwittingly into the middle of a turf war between warring factions. Director Darren Aronofskys comedy thriller, starring Austin Butler, Zoe Kravitz and Matt Smith

programme.title: Roofman (2025)
programme.desc: Former US Army Reserve officer Jeffrey Manchester executes a plan to rob multiple branches of McDonalds. He is eventually captured by police and becomes the first person to escape from Brown Creek Correctional Institution in North Carolina. Jeffrey hides out at a toy shop for several months, and during forays outside the building, he sparks romance with store worker Leigh Wainscott. Comedy drama, starring Channing Tatum, Kirsten Dunst and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - May 2026
programme.desc: A look ahead at the films to enjoy this month on Sky Cinema

programme.title: Flavia (2025)

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw acquires an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennells drama based on Emily Brontes romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Murders That Shocked the Nation
programme.desc: The presenter explores the case of John Straffen, who killed two young girls in the summer of 1951. He was found to be unfit to plead and committed to Broadmoor Hospital, but during a brief escape from the facility in 1952 he killed again. It was determined that the cause of his behaviour was severe brain damage, traced back to an attack of encephalitis as a child in India. When he died in 2007, he was the longest-serving prisoner in Britain

programme.title: Mr Dynamite: The Rise of James Brown
programme.desc: The career of the singer, musician and producer, charting his journey from his first hit Please, Please, Please in 1956 through his pioneering funk phase to later chart success

programme.title: My Icon
programme.desc: The former England cricket star and World Cup winner discusses the individuals that have inspired her in her life and career

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of actress Joan Crawford, who won an Academy Award for her role in the 1945 film Mildred Pierce and was nominated for Possessed and Sudden Fear

programme.title: The Movies
programme.desc: The history of American cinema continues with the 1960s, tracing the impact of movies including West Side Story and The Graduate

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the silent film pioneer, whose stone-faced physical comedy turned him into a Hollywood superstar in the 1920s

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American director Oliver Stone, whose films include JFK, Nixon and Wall Street and who won Oscars for Platoon and Born on the Fourth of July

programme.desc: While Tonkas fate becomes clearer, Tonia struggles to let go and dreams of opening her own island sanctuary where she can live among chimpanzees

programme.desc: An examination of evidence that surrounds Lee Harvey Oswald, and his associations with groups linked to the CIA

programme.title: The Nineties
programme.desc: A look at terrorism during the decade, from the Unabomber and the explosion at the Centennial Olympic Park, to the Oklahoma City bombing and Columbine

programme.title: Germanwings: What Happened on Flight 9525?

programme.title: Im Chevy Chase and Youre Not
programme.desc: Chevy Chase was a cast member on the inaugural series of Saturday Night Live and he built on that success to star in numerous Hollywood comedies and host the Academy Awards. He played hapless father Clark Griswold in the National Lampoon films and ran amok in pictures including Caddyshack, Spies Like Us, Fletch and Three Amigos. Two-time Emmy Award winner Marina Zenovich directs an authorised feature-length documentary portrait of Chase, which includes rare and intimate home movies and archive footage. Chase and his family share memories directly to camera, enriched with contributions from friends and collaborators including Dan Aykroyd, Beverly DAngelo, Goldie Hawn, Lorne Michaels, Ryan Reynolds and Martin Short.

programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts treks across Britain, decoding the truth behind its Holy Grail legends, linking Arthurian lore, sacred relics and the Knights Templar. From the Isle of Wight to Glastonbury, Oxford, London and Scotland, she meets passionate locals, unearths legends of sacred vessels and ancient cauldrons and investigates Arthurs grave, where the trail takes an interesting turn

programme.title: Lost Grail with Alice Roberts

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. Biologist Patrick Aryee explores how cats have evolved throughout the millennia, learning to thrive in inhospitable environments, as well as becoming the worlds most popular pet. In the first edition there is spectacular footage of caracals jumping three metres to catch flying birds, and a rare Bornean bay cat, which was thought to have been extinct. Patrick finds out how tigers made the transition from South-east Asias steamy jungles to the -40C temperatures of Siberia, and meets lion whisperer Kevin Richardson
