The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman brings in Teddy the shih-tzu cross, who has severely deformed front legs. His owner wants him to live a life without pain, but coming up with a surgical solution proves a challenge. Five-year-old golden retriever Ollie is referred to Fitzpatricks for an operation to remove a tumour in his leg, and a young couple bring in their four-month-old bulldog, which suffered a fractured elbow after wriggling from their arms

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman travels all the way from Romania in the hope that Professor Noel Fitzpatrick can mend her dog Cliffords squashed spinal cord. A golden Labrador is offered a total shoulder replacement and a puppy faces losing two of its toes after a collision with a plant pot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles heads to Duxford in Cambridgeshire, where he enlists the help of local tradespeople to assist Paul and Sarah Behan. The couple had started on an extension to their small bungalow when the project had to be abandoned due to their son Joshua being diagnosed with a rare and aggressive form of liver cancer. The team puts in an impressive effort, but with only nine days to finish the job the challenge is extensive

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles heads to Maidstone, Kent, to meet former Royal Engineer and paratrooper Mo Morris. After 20 years in the service, he was given a medical discharge due to damage to his knees, and has been left struggling to walk unaided ever since. At present, his home is unsuited to his needs, putting pressure on his relatives as they try to help as best they can. Determined to give him back his independence, the DIY SOS team rallies the local troops, including tradespeople and neighbours, to work together and adapt Mos house, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to make sure everything goes to plan

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete helps a withdrawn pregnant Malinois regain strength so she can care for her 11 lively pups, and falls for Winter, a Staffie who is covered in scars

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Expectant mother Hayley has been anxious since her 20-week scan, which revealed her baby has a rare type of hernia that could restrict the development of its lungs. She and partner Pete have been unable to plan for their childs future as the condition is potentially life-threatening, but they try to remain positive throughout the labour, knowing that the most difficult period will come immediately after the birth

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Christian couple Judith and Mike put their faith in both God and the medical staff when things dont go to plan in the delivery room, and as 17-year-old Beths labour develops, her partner Dominic becomes more and more anxious. However, the expectant father risks missing out on the birth of his child by going for a smoke in the hospital car park

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Nessa goes into labour a month early in Barry, and the newlyweds must forget their differences to track down Smithy in time for the birth of his child. Meanwhile, Stacey returns to Essex to salvage her marriage

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Gavin starts his job in Cardiff and, while Stacey is thrilled to be back in Wales, Smithy has to come to terms with living in Essex without his best friend. Nessa has taken up residence in Daves caravan in Sully and is getting ready for Neils christening. The event means a big reunion for the gang, at which Smithy receives surprising news from Nessa, and Stacey has a special request for her husband

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Zak treats a 90-year-old woman after paramedics gave her CPR, Tristan gets a diagnosis spot-on, and Christine has a patient with an alarming combination of symptoms

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team help the Clark family declutter as they lay out their belongings in a warehouse only to discover they own 521 odd socks, 75 barbies and 28 games controllers. After moving from the US to a much smaller house in the UK the family no longer have the space they need. Carpenter Rob creates some floor to ceiling storage for the family while Dilly reorganises the kitchen and laundry area

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Flower, the first dog ever to take a dislike to him . He also helps Heidi and Marvin, both facing serious medical challenges

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Since his wife died shortly after giving birth to their sixth child, Paul Makings has been valiantly holding the family together as they grieve. But hes facing even more problems - living in a two-bedroom house he has started to extend, and forced to sleep on the sofa. Nick Knowles and the team are joined by more than 100 locals in Misterton, Nottinghamshire, as they take on their biggest home build ever. However, they have to battle against a blizzard to complete the job in nine days

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of Manchester couple Rebecca and Steve, whose life was changed for ever when they found out they were expecting triplets. Two years on, Rebecca is becoming depressed by the state of their small, damp, dilapidated house, which is unsafe for the children. The builders have just nine days to bring the property up to date and give this tired mum and dad the space they need for their active offspring. Luckily, local tradespeople and other members of the community turn out in force to help

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Spalding, Lincolnshire, to meet Dakota Read and her family. The eight-year-old was born with quadriplegic cerebral palsy and her home needs to be made more wheelchair-friendly. Enter the DIY SOS builders, who set to work on designer Gabrielle Blackmans vision, although with just nine days to complete the task, theyll need help. Luckily, the local tradespeople are also out in force, giving their time and their skills for free

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Pioneering veterinarian Noel Fitzpatrick uses his talents to treat a puppy called Poppy, which has difficulties with walking, and a lurcher called Dylan needs help with a collapsed joint. The programme also features a one-year-old cat that was hit by a car on its first venture outside the house, leaving it requiring emergency treatment

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat Staffordshire bull terrier Lulu, who has been hit by a car and had her pelvis smashed into more than 25 pieces. The professors only option is to pin the larger pieces back together in an operation that will require all his expertise. Noel and his team also treat a giant schnauzer called Florence who has a sore knee and a pointy-eared Jack Russell called Muffin who has a mysterious problem with her back legs

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Since his wife died shortly after giving birth to their sixth child, Paul Makings has been valiantly holding the family together as they grieve. programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The teams stand by for casualties as police launch an armed raid on the home of a wanted man. In Bradford a minibus carrying children with special needs is in collision with a stolen car

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildons Dogs Trust rehoming centre. Its Petes first day at Dogs Trust, and he meets a pug facing a worrying surgery and pregnant dog who was seized at the border

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets a litter of puppies who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Bryn is in his element as he awaits the arrival of the Essex boys, who are having a big night out in Cardiff and staying for a sleepover, but Smithy is concerned about seeing Nessa again after their last meeting. Meanwhile, Stacey tells Gavin about a worrying discovery, and Pam and Mick discuss their plans for a new conservatory

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Leslie Phillips and Olympic curling champion Rhona Martin guest star in the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky characters. Among other highlights, Nan visits an old friend in hospital and Lauren takes a job in a burger bar

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Dave is unhappy about Nessas plans to let Smithy see the baby, while Stacey ensures Gavin sticks to his promise as she tries to find a new job. Meanwhile, Dawn and Pete join Pam and Mick for a beer and curry night. Comedy, starring Joanna Page and Mathew Horne

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics help a man suffering from anxiety who discovered he might have been exposed to HIV. Crews attend to a man found unconscious in the stairwell of a block of flats

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz and the team come to the aid of former firefighter Pa Berry, who suffered extensive injuries in the line of duty and is in need of help in completing his home renovation

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Zak treats a 90-year-old woman after paramedics gave her CPR, Tristan gets a diagnosis spot-on, and Christine has a patient with an alarming combination of symptoms

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey coaches the Smith family through a mammoth declutter as their possessions are laid out in a warehouse as though it were an art installation they discover 207 loose nappies, 145 Punjabi suits and 24 old toothbrushes amongst other things. Things get emotional as Raaj lets go of some of her late sisters belongings whom she lost to Covid. Carpenter Rob works hard to make their three-bedroom house accommodate six people and Dilly creates a brand-new larder while reorganising the kitchen

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat Staffordshire bull terrier Lulu, who has been hit by a car and had her pelvis smashed into more than 25 pieces. The professors only option is to pin the larger pieces back together in an operation that will require all his expertise. Noel and his team also treat a giant schnauzer called Florence who has a sore knee and a pointy-eared Jack Russell called Muffin who has a mysterious problem with her back legs

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Since his wife died shortly after giving birth to their sixth child, Paul Makings has been valiantly holding the family together as they grieve. But hes facing even more problems - living in a two-bedroom house he has started to extend, and forced to sleep on the sofa. Nick Knowles and the team are joined by more than 100 locals in Misterton, Nottinghamshire, as they take on their biggest home build ever. However, they have to battle against a blizzard to complete the job in nine days

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of Manchester couple Rebecca and Steve, whose life was changed for ever when they found out they were expecting triplets. Two years on, Rebecca is becoming depressed by the state of their small, damp, dilapidated house, which is unsafe for the children. The builders have just nine days to bring the property up to date and give this tired mum and dad the space they need for their active offspring. Luckily, local tradespeople and other members of the community turn out in force to help

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The teams stand by for casualties as police launch an armed raid on the home of a wanted man. In Bradford a minibus carrying children with special needs is in collision with a stolen car

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildons Dogs Trust rehoming centre. Its Petes first day at Dogs Trust, and he meets a pug facing a worrying surgery and pregnant dog who was seized at the border

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets a litter of puppies who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Nessa goes into labour a month early in Barry, and the newlyweds must forget their differences to track down Smithy in time for the birth of his child. Meanwhile, Stacey returns to Essex to salvage her marriage

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Gavin starts his job in Cardiff and, while Stacey is thrilled to be back in Wales, Smithy has to come to terms with living in Essex without his best friend. Nessa has taken up residence in Daves caravan in Sully and is getting ready for Neils christening. The event means a big reunion for the gang, at which Smithy receives surprising news from Nessa, and Stacey has a special request for her husband

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Jools Holland and pop band the Divine Comedy guest star in the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and bizarre characters. Lauren fights with Kelly over Ryan, and 1950s landlady Ma Willow makes her first appearance

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren receives a proposal, and Bernies sisters make an appearance, while Derek Faye meets Bonnie Langford, guest starring as herself

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics help a man suffering from anxiety who discovered he might have been exposed to HIV. Crews attend to a man found unconscious in the stairwell of a block of flats

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz and the team come to the aid of former firefighter Pa Berry, who suffered extensive injuries in the line of duty and is in need of help in completing his home renovation

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: Plants that have developed to thrive in the desert, including cacti that grow in the shade of other trees and collect water in pleated trunks that expand and contract - but can also find themselves a host to other plants, like desert mistletoe. The programme also reveals how tobacco plants being eaten by caterpillars are able to summon the creatures natural predators, and how tumbleweeds roll across the landscape, only unfurling and growing when they encounter rain

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus splash out £550 on a garage owners Mercedes 190. Its previous owner has failed to fix it, but the duo are more confident

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien finds a barn containing a collection of replica Rolls-Royce cars, and Henry Cole tries his hand at driving a tractor as they continue their money-making tour

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: A new £12million pelagic boat presents trouble for Willie and Peter Tait when its state-of-the-art communications system breaks down. Meanwhile, James Buchan finds his cheaper method, using a delicate seine net, has its own risks as he searches for fish among wrecks on the seabed

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: On board Amity, Jimmy Buchan lands a hefty catch of prawns and wrestles with fears for his own safety and for the lives of his crew. On the Fruitful Harvest, Sandy swaps places with his 18-year-old apprentice in a role-reversal, which turns out to be an eye-opening experience. Later, when the boats return home to Peterhead, they get a shock that nobody could have anticipated

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty 1913 Bradshaws Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo travels east through the Balkans along the most exotic section of the route taken by the Orient Express. His journey begins in Sofia, capital of Bulgaria, and includes stops in the ancient city of Plovdiv, the region of Rumelia, and former capital of the Ottoman Empire Edirne. Arriving in Istanbul, Michael takes a trip on the Bosphorus - a much-coveted stretch of water - finds out about Turkish delight and travels along the Marmaray metro line, which connects European and Asian Istanbul via a tunnel beneath the Bosphorus

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy uncovers the story of Edward IVs sons Edward and Richard, whose disappearance in 1483 has led to centuries of mystery. The princes were taken to the Tower of London for their own protection, but a priest declared the boys illegitimate and their uncle Richard next in line to the throne. The gaps in the historical record have fuelled 500 years of speculation, so Lucy speaks to history professor Tim Thornton, who has found evidence of one account of what happened written by Thomas More, while Richard III Society chair Matthew Lewis gives his views on the events

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team lift the shutters on their biggest restoration to date in the shape of a Series 1 Land Rover. But does that mean record profit or history making loss?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team fly the flag for King and country with one of their most luxurious purchases to date - a 1981 Rolls-Royce Silver Spirit

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How Japans early victories over Allied forces during the Second World War convinced its people that their strength and discipline would bring them victory. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How Second World War criminal Adolf Eichmann was tracked down to Buenos Aires, Argentina, in 1961

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce pays a final visit to Alexandra Gardens in Cardiff, where treasures include a rare Welsh rugby shirt, colourful cameo glass and unseen photos of the Rolling Stones. She also meets a man who is helping to preserve the history of traditional Welsh music, and tries to work out which piece of colourful cameo glass is the most valuable

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings, filmed in Normandy, France, and the D-Day Museum Portsmouth. The story of the landings are told through various items brought in by visitors and rare artefacts held in various museums across the UK, and there are eyewitness accounts from those who played a part, including radio operator Marie Scott and veteran Ken Scott

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Anglophile Derek struggles to get his message across on a rare trip abroad to pick up a Bentley, while a well-restored Ford XR2 sets the auction alight

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave finds a very rare 70s Sunbeam Lotus that has been rotting away in a farmyard for 29 years, while a Morris Minor convertible goes under the hammer

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How Japans early victories over Allied forces during the Second World War convinced its people that their strength and discipline would bring them victory. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How Second World War criminal Adolf Eichmann was tracked down to Buenos Aires, Argentina, in 1961

programme.title: Frozen Planet II
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores frozen regions around the world. The first episode begins in Antarctica, where Emperor Penguin chicks are abandoned by their parents as spring begins and must find their way across the treacherous sea ice to the rich waters of the Southern Ocean. In the Himalayas, the worlds fluffiest cat hunts for gerbils and voles, while in the boreal forests circling North America, Europe and Asia, a Siberian tiger is on the lookout for black bears

programme.title: Frozen Planet II
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a glimpse of the wildlife of the Arctic Ocean, as spring begins after four months of winter darkness. Polar bears lead their cubs to hunt, beluga whales trapped by ice see the possibility of escape, harp seals teach their pups to swim, and bowhead whales come in search of food. Plus, a look at how the region is warming faster than anywhere else on Earth due to climate change

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has to extract a vintage car from its bizarre resting place, while Charlie picks up a Vauxhall Viva from a man who has owned it for over half a century

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Up for sale at the Mathewsons auction - a 1275 Mini GT thats come from just down the road that could be a future British icon

programme.title: Resto My Ride
programme.sub-title: Were Going to Bash this Holden
programme.desc: Sam commits the crew to work on a HR Holden in three weeks so she can go on the Variety Bash and drive from Melbourne to Darwin for charity.

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: A guide to plants that thrive in human-dominated environments, including trees that grow across canyons in India, which have developed into living bridges. The programme examines the origins of growing crops and the ongoing influence on the world of wild plants, as well as efforts to preserve species, from snipers firing pesticide bullets from helicopters to fend off invasive plants to plants being painstakingly pollinated using a paintbrush

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus take on a Mazda MX-5 thats been given some real boy racer attitude, hoping to return it to its former glory

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Force 11 storms are expected in Peterhead, Aberdeenshire, meaning the fishing fleet is safely in harbour with just two boats out at sea. Ocean Ventures skipper John D Buchan is prepared to prove a point to his father and sail to the coast of Norway. Meanwhile, 30 miles off the Shetlands, Charlie and Charles McBride brave the weather to repay their debts

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Father-and-son Charlie and Charles McBride have a disastrous week as they lose their fishing net and their boats hydraulic power is reduced. Jimmy Buchanan has worries of his own when he learns his engine will cost £70,000 to repair. Meanwhile, Chaz Bruce, the youngest skipper in Fraserburghs prawn fleet, braves force eight gales

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo experiences first-hand Italys need for speed in a state-of-the-art Maserati sports car, and discovers how the leaning tower of Pisa was rescued from near collapse. In Carrara, he finds out how the marble used by Michelangelo is still being quarried and is invited to chip away at a contemporary sculpture. Moving on to Bologna, the former politician embarks on a doomed search for spaghetti bolognese before being taught how to prepare a tagliatelle al ragu. Finally, he lets the wind rush through his hair on a high-speed boat trip across Lake Garda

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George IIIs mental illness changed Britain, uncovering royal papers and exploring how the attempt on the kings life by a mentally ill subject transformed psychiatry forever

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team think they can smell profit by choosing one of the simplest cars ever built, the VW Beetle

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team opt to buy one of the most affordable sports cars on the road - the Mazda MX-5

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Allied offensive in the Pacific, where Americas two-pronged attack led to heavy casualties on both sides, as the Japanese mounted a ferocious defence. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight on the Beaches
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the coastal defence network built to defend Britains beaches, including a tower in Essex which controlled mines laid across the estuary

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts visit Bolton Abbey, near Skipton in North Yorkshire, where discoveries include a farmers lambing chair, a small Faberge trinket and a collection of items chronicling the life of a First World War aviator

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team travel to Althorp, Northamptonshire, childhood home and burial place of Diana, Princess of Wales, and residence of the Spencer family for more than 500 years. Fiona Bruce talks to Charles Spencer about living among reminders of the estates history, and he seizes on the opportunity to ask for advice about one of his treasures. Other pieces valued include a walnut desk and a collection of paintings

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A delightful collection of British classics comes up for auction, including an Austin A35 and a Hillman Avenger Estate in stunning condition. An E-Type Jaguar also hits the auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Three MGs from the 70s turn up at the garage, still covered in original factory wax. Nobody can believe it, or value them. A Morris 1000 turns up at the auction along with a TR7 V8

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Allied offensive in the Pacific, where Americas two-pronged attack led to heavy casualties on both sides, as the Japanese mounted a ferocious defence. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight on the Beaches
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the coastal defence network built to defend Britains beaches, including a tower in Essex which controlled mines laid across the estuary

programme.title: Frozen Planet II
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to wildlife that have adapted to survive in frozen mountain regions. On the high slopes of Mount Kenya, a pregnant High Casqued Chameleon must choose the right time in a daily cycle of tropical sun and frost at night to give birth. The mountains of Japan are the snowiest place on Earth, providing hostile conditions for a lone male Macaque cast out of his troop. In the remote Southern Alps of New Zealand, parrots feed on the dead, while in the Andes, flamingos thrive in high altitude volcanic lakes

programme.title: Frozen Planet II
programme.desc: The wildlife of Antarctica, including king penguins that must evade leopard seals to feed at sea, albatrosses responding to diminishing number of females in their population and rare footage of the Antarctic blue whale, the largest animal to have ever lived. As the programme ventures into the heart of the continent, surprising footage reveals one of the most volcanic regions on Earth, with unexpected sand dunes hidden in a rare ice-free valley

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber takes up boxing and enters the prison championships. He is hot favourite to win until Fletcher is given the task of fixing the contest and nobbling his cell-mate. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Peter Vaughan

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Harris accidentally drops stolen pills into Fletchers midday cuppa - much to the annoyance of prison racketeer Harry Grout, who resents a newcomer muscling in on his patch. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker. First broadcast in 1977

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher objects to having a third cellmate foisted on him, especially as the newcomer seems to enjoy special privileges and the full backing of the wardens. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in freshwater environments, from gliding treefrogs engaged in fiercely competitive mating rituals in in the Costa Rican rainforest to mugger crocodiles in Sri Lanka, that lie in wait at waterholes for chital deer. In the Okavango Delta, the arrival of the great annual flood poses a significant challenge for a pack of five African wild dogs

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: The A4 LNER Pacific Locomotive, Sir Nigel Gresley, must be hauled 46 miles at 30mph from Crewe to Bridgnorth

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus reckon theres big profit to be made when they track down a 1980s Austin Metro. Its cheap, its clean and its bright yellow and they have high hopes to treble their money

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Inexperienced skipper Chaz Bruce and his crew run into difficulty when a rope wraps around the boats propeller and the engine loses revs, but he is determined to prove himself. Meanwhile, disaster strikes for Jimmy Buchan, and captain Alan Watt heads for the North Atlantic

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Insight into the dangerous job of John Alexander, who works alone on his tiny boat, fishing in all weather for brown crabs. Elsewhere, Alan Watt returns from the North Atlantic with a lucrative haul of monkfish, but his journey is put in jeopardy when the main winch jams. On the New Dawn, Chaz Bruce endures a nightmare trip

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Harris accidentally drops stolen pills into Fletchers midday cuppa - much to the annoyance of prison racketeer Harry Grout, who resents a newcomer muscling in on his patch. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker. First broadcast in 1977

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher objects to having a third cellmate foisted on him, especially as the newcomer seems to enjoy special privileges and the full backing of the wardens. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in freshwater environments, from gliding treefrogs engaged in fiercely competitive mating rituals in in the Costa Rican rainforest to mugger crocodiles in Sri Lanka, that lie in wait at waterholes for chital deer. In the Okavango Delta, the arrival of the great annual flood poses a significant challenge for a pack of five African wild dogs

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: A guide to plants that thrive in human-dominated environments, including trees that grow across canyons in India, which have developed into living bridges. The programme examines the origins of growing crops and the ongoing influence on the world of wild plants, as well as efforts to preserve species, from snipers firing pesticide bullets from helicopters to fend off invasive plants to plants being painstakingly pollinated using a paintbrush

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Inexperienced skipper Chaz Bruce and his crew run into difficulty when a rope wraps around the boats propeller and the engine loses revs, but he is determined to prove himself. Meanwhile, disaster strikes for Jimmy Buchan, and captain Alan Watt heads for the North Atlantic

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Insight into the dangerous job of John Alexander, who works alone on his tiny boat, fishing in all weather for brown crabs. programme.desc: A young woman who stumbled upon devastating secret footage hidden on her own computer, which left her heartbroken and traumatised. This programme follows Julianas quest for truth and justice

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe learns the result of her pregnancy test and the friends competitive spirit gets the better of them in a battle of the sexes arising from an apparently simple challenge. Comedy, starring Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy contemplates his future, while Dawn finds herself blindsided and Todd continues to be manipulative

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo plays cat-and-mouse with Todd, Megans web of lies begins to unravel, Ronnie takes matters into his own hands, Summer and Sarah get caught in the crossfire, and Betsy makes a sickening discovery

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Graham Norton makes a surprise visit to the triumphant alliance and delivers a game-changing decision. The neighbours must agree, but will they see eye-to-eye? A friendly village BBQ explodes into a shouting match when tensions boil over, and one neighbour has a few choice words for everyone. With a war brewing between two houses, everyone is feeling the heat as the close heads for a dramatic third exit

programme.title: My Boyfriend, My Rapist: The Secret Footage
programme.desc: A young woman who stumbled upon devastating secret footage hidden on her own computer, which left her heartbroken and traumatised. Thirty-two year old Juliana Terlizzi discovered a set of videos of herself laying drugged and unconscious in her own bed, while her then-partner Hubert Greliak sexually assaulted her and filmed the act. This programme follows Julianas quest for truth and justice

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders
programme.desc: Drama based on the true story of John Cooper, a serial killer who evaded justice for more than 20 years. Two unsolved double murders from the 1980s cast a shadow over the work of the Dyfed Powys police force, so in 2006, newly promoted Detective Superintendent Steve Wilkins decides to reopen both cases. Starring Luke Evans, Keith Allen and Alexandria Riley

programme.title: The Beckham Feud: Truth and Lies
programme.desc: Documentary examining the explosive rift inside Britains most famous family, following a public statement from Brooklyn Beckham on Instagram. Journalists who have tracked the family for decades investigate the ways in which fame, loyalty and public scrutiny have strained a once carefully managed family brand

programme.title: The Widower
programme.desc: DS Henry raids Malcolms home after hearing hes embezzling funds at an angling club, and finds stolen laptops as well as an unlicensed firearm - but legal technicalities result in the case being dropped. The detective then discovers Malcolm and Simone are planning a transatlantic trip and fears the killer could use this opportunity to murder his fiancee. Will he be able to warn her before its too late? Fact-based drama, starring Reece Shearsmith, John Hannah and Archie Panjabi

programme.title: The Suspect
programme.desc: A recent life-changing medical diagnosis has thrown psychologist Joe OLoughlin off-balance, and he is feeling reckless. When a young woman is found murdered, the unusual manner of her death prompts DS Riya Devi to suggest asking Joe for a professional opinion. Joe is shocked by the sight of the body - but is he just shaken by the brutality of the killing or is there something from his past hes not telling the police? Thriller, starring Aidan Turner, Shaun Parkes and Anjli Mohindra

programme.title: McDonald and Dodds
programme.desc: Having just tied up a case after a local scandal-breaking journalist died of anaphylaxis at a party in her home, the detectives investigate when a woman is found dead in a rented flat. The killer has removed all clues to the victims identity, but DNA reveals that shes a missing person who vanished on her way home from school more than 35 years ago. Can McDonald and Dodds navigate the tightly wound social circles of Baths elites to find the truth? Crime drama, starring Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff fails to control of a violent outbreak at the bookmakers, and is in awe of Joe as he takes command of the situation. Debt collector Mick Revill (guest star Martin Kemp) suggests a way to toughen him up, but elsewhere Revill is accused by a local businessman of being involved in some dubious deals. Meanwhile, Peggy cant understand Davids reluctance to attend a centenary party at the school

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirots advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The next generation of engineering wonders, beginning with an international team in Norway who are building the largest polar expedition vessel in the world

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare classic comfort foods, starting with vegetarian lasagne and an oozy cheesy garlic swirl, followed by a spicy chicken traybake served with pommes Anna. John also whips up sweet and sour prawns and noodles, and Lisa makes her dads favourite dessert - treacle tart with a special topping

programme.title: My Unique BandB
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team create an unusual BandB in Devon for Michele and Tobin, who want to turn their tired summerhouse into a property that could accommodate four-legged guests

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces, this time focusing on the impact of the pandemic at the Royal Free London, a world leader in the treatment of infectious diseases and one of the biggest NHS trusts in the country. In the first edition, the UK lockdown is over, but hospital staff are counting the cost of having to prioritise Covid-19 above almost everything else as they battle to treat the patients left behind

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Emily finds out she is pregnant, but the joy is tempered by ill-feeling from the Jacobs family over the death of their father, who was run over by a tractor. Meanwhile, Kate enjoys peculiar cravings. Sixties-based drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a young couple desperate to start a family against all odds and a brave father hoping to give his young daughter the gift of life for the second time. She also finds out about a single mum looking for love, an IT manager pledging to take to the stage and a lady who wants nothing more than to be able to hear her childrens voices

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Eastbourne, where she tries to help owner Judith give The Sheldon a new identity to make it stand out from the crowd in an area of stiff competition. Alex needs Judith to decide on what she wants the establishment to be if she is going to get The Sheldon shipshape before the all-important summer season, and suggests a family-friendly BandB is the way to go

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain has to face his fears, while Moira opens up to Chas and Pollard has a word with Cain

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jodie comes to the rescue when her dodgy past catches up with Shona. But when Shona inadvertently reveals a betrayal of her own, a furious Jodie targets David in a bid to break up her sisters marriage

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Burnett is called out to a dilapidated farmhouse, where a teenager is running a high fever and his family seems to be struggling to cope. As the staff try to diagnose the youngsters illness, his pregnant mother is rushed to the hospital with the same symptoms and is left fighting for her life. Meanwhile, Ormerod and Rose attend to Sir Edward Fawcett, an eccentric explorer who wants to hike around Mexico, despite having a condition that means his days of adventure are over. Medical drama, guest starring Brian Blessed and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack investigates the abduction of a bus driver and his conductor and an unrelated murder of a childrens entertainer, but is frustrated to find himself working alongside a former colleague who got him suspended by making a complaint about his handling of a case. Long-running crime drama, with David Jason, and co-starring Cherie Lunghi and John Lyons

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates graffiti at a battery egg farm, and discovers there is more to the case than meets the eye. Meanwhile, Greengrass attracts suspicion by selling free-range eggs. Police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: North Shore
programme.desc: When a personal encounter inadvertently helps Meg locate the crime scene, the detectives appear to have the evidence they need to lay charges - however, their suspects are represented by the same lawyers and they tell identical stories, except for one small change, that are all designed to hang Oscar out to dry. Max and Meg appeal to Oscar not to become a scapegoat for the Macklins, but will it be the whole truth when Oscar decides to tell them what really happened?

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: After a clash with the Battersby sisters fails to gain Daniels sympathy, Megan sets her sights on blackmailing Maggie over her late partners demise, leading to a tense showdown between the two women

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn finds herself questioning everything, while Todd plays manipulative games and Paddy hopes Simo can help

programme.title: Safe Harbour

programme.title: The Widower

Despite it being the busiest route of the day, there isnt a single eyewitness to his death, and the detectives face a mammoth challenge, with their only clue a ticket stub for a local arts and culture festival. Crime drama, starring Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An explosion in the woods is linked to the activities of a villain working out of a local quarry. Rachel suspects the owner of supplying dynamite to armed robbery gangs, so Joe goes undercover to infiltrate the workforce - but ends up in mortal danger when his cover is blown. Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Poirot meets a woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The race to build a massive new rollercoaster in Canadas biggest theme park before it opens its gates for the summer - a ride with a record-breaking 90-degree vertical drop

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a three-course menu perfect for a romantic evening. For starters, there is pork rilette served with a garlic and cheese bread, while the main course is salmon en croute, with a side order of hasselback root vegetables, peas and beetroot and a fresh horseradish sauce. For dessert, Lisa prepares a marble chocolate and orange bundt cake with chocolate sauce

programme.title: My Unique BandB
programme.desc: In Monmouthshire in South Wales, Simon Parfett and his team take on a 1980s double decker bus conversion for recycling centre worker Stacy

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: It is mid-summer and the Royal Free London NHS Hospital Foundation Trust faces the long-lasting effects of Covid-19, with departments across the trust battling for scarce theatre slots while trying to prioritise their volume of cases. At Barnet Hospital, a large team meeting is underway when a clinician is informed he has tested positive for coronavirus and must leave the site immediately, while the infection control team springs into action to identify who has had significant contact with him and must therefore also go home and isolate for 14 days

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates graffiti at a battery egg farm, and discovers there is more to the case than meets the eye. Meanwhile, Greengrass attracts suspicion by selling free-range eggs. Police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

Plus, a schoolboy with dreams of becoming a professional footballer, and a young father desperate to thank the kind stranger who saved his life

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi has been called to help Alan, tenanted landlord of the Crown Inn, nestled in the Wiltshire village of Aldbourne. Seven years ago, Alan took on the Crown hoping it would set him up for retirement. But now at 69 years old, he is still working every hour of every day just to get by. With the Crown failing to pull any profit, no pension in place and no Plan B, Alan is desperate for Alexs help

With the timeline of Julie Anns injuries not adding up, Jane pursues a witness who say Eddie visited George Collins the night before her death, while Clifford Bentley is released from prison and returns home to a heros welcome
